entry.title: Extract Workday® HCM data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Once again, we are expanding our offering to analyze processes that touch non-SAP system – this time for Human Resources. We are happy to announce the release of our new integration package content for Workday® Human Capital Management which allows you to extract data from Workday® HCM into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

Improve Your Human Resource Process Analysis

Improved and streamlined HR processes have a positive lasting impact on your business efficiency and benefits your workforce. The new integration content for Workday® HCM and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence is an essential starting point and enables you to visualize, analyze, and get fast insights into your Attract to Acquire process.

Access the Integration Content on Business Accelerator Hub

Let’s dive into the topic and start with where you can find the integration content.

Logon to SAP Business Accelerator Hub and under "Explore" search for "SAP Signavio": 

 

Scroll down until you see the “Discover Integrations” area and search for Workday®.

 

Click on the Workday® tile. There you can find the available Workday® integration package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. 

Click on the integration package.

 

You see a list of integration flows highlighting which business objects and events are being extracted. This includes the business objects "Job Application", "Employee Contract", "Candidate", and many more.

For further details on the underlying data extraction logic from Workday HCM, how it is transformed and pushed into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – you can open the respective integration flows.

 

Also check out the configuration guide attached to the integration package.

The integration package includes dedicated integration flows that outline the relevant business objects and events extracted by default. This setup enables a quick start with the flexibility to adjust and extend the content to meet your specific business needs.

 

Download content into SAP Integration Suite

To use the content, log on to SAP Integration Suite and go to “Discover” and click on the "Integrations" section. 

 

Use the search bar and search for "Workday", you will find all available content relating to Workday integration, including our new package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence:

 

If you check out the package "Workday Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence", you can find details on all available artifacts on the integration scenarios. To use the package, simply click on "copy" on the top right corner. This will automatically copy this integration package into your design area for further configuration and deployment.

 

Key Benefits of the Integration

  • Accelerated Connectivity Setup: Speed up the integration between Workday HCM and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence with pre-identified objects and fields that simplify the initial setup.
  • Easily Adjustable Content: The delivered content can be adapted to your organization’s requirements, ensuring flexibility while reducing implementation effort.
  • Faster Insights: The streamlined setup allows you to analyze hiring operations more quickly, uncover inefficiencies, and support evidence-based decision-making.

Share Your Feedback

We are looking forward to hear your thoughts and feedback on the new integration package. Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments section below.

In case you missed it – check out the available integration content for Salesforce (Blog post / Integration content), Microsoft Dynamics CRM (Blog post / Integration content) and Coupa (Blog post / Integration content).

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q2 ’25)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to bring you the latest updates and maintain the momentum of delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, explore the newest content packages released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q2 2025, along with a high-level overview of the year’s progress so far.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration package published:

Oracle Inventory Management System Integration with SAP S/4HANA

The Oracle Inventory Management System Integration with SAP S/4HANA package in SAP Cloud Integration facilitates seamless data exchange between Oracle Inventory Management System and SAP S/4HANA.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration package published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Integration with SAP Ariba Procurement

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Buy-side adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow into the buy-side process. New Contract Draft Requests (CDR) created on Sirion CLM are automatically transitioned into a Purchase Requisition (PR) on SAP Ariba enabling a sync between two systems for the entire Buy-side and contracting flow.

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management with SAP Ariba Sourcing Awards

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Sourcing Adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow into the sourcing process. Sourcing Projects and Events initiated in SAP Ariba are automatically transitioned into Contract Requests (CDRs) and Contract Line Items (CLIs) in Sirion CLM, enabling synchronization between the two systems throughout the sourcing and contracting flow.​

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management with SAP Ariba Sourcing Bids

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Sourcing Adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow into the sourcing process. Sourcing Projects and Events initiated in SAP Ariba are automatically transitioned into Contract Draft Requests (CDRs) and Contract Line Items (CLIs) in Sirion CLM, enabling synchronization between the two systems throughout the sourcing and contracting flow.​

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

LTIMindtree Limited

Validated Partner Use Case package published:

Intelligent Master Data Curator

Intelligent Master Data Curator transforms SAP Master Data Governance by harnessing the power of advanced Gen AI and Narrow AI technologies. By automating the validation of global attributes and accurately proposing local attributes, this solution ensures unparalleled data quality and integrity.

Elevating Supply Chain Performance with SmartATP

The implementation of Gen AI empowers organizations to anticipate potential delays from suppliers or vendors well in advance. This foresight enables the revision of production and sales plans, allowing for timely action to minimize disruptions. Gen AI can also trigger advanced notifications to planners and sales teams, ensuring proactive measures are taken to mitigate delays effectively.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ATOS INTERNATIONAL SAS

Validated Partner Use Case package published:

QE Assist for Digital Assurance

AI powered QE Assist - where Quality Assurance meets the power of intelligence, is the game changing solution that combines predictive AI and natural language capability driven by trained large language models (LLM) to offer a seamless, efficient, and impactful experience.

AI based Maintenance Assistant in SAP EAM

GenAI based knowledge assistant for maintenance and service technicians using large language Model via SAP BTP on SAP PM Objects.

Knowledge Pilot powered by GenAI

When the information is stored in multiple systems, multiple documents and multiple locations, search cost gets higher and efficiency reduces. Knowledge Pilot is an AI-powered knowledge search tool that provides precise, accurate, grounded information you need quickly and easily. The solution can be utilized for any situation and scenario which require high volume of documentation or huge knowledge repositories, but needs faster search capability.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BIGCube GmbH

Validated Partner Use Case package published:

Streamlined Custom Table Maintenance

The application is based on an ABAP back end deployed in an SAP BW/4HANA system and offers odata services to connect to the front end. The app is a kind of framework that makes it possible to manage data in tables as desired. With granular privilege control (SAP Standard Authorization objects), users can only modify data they're authorized to, ensuring security and compliance.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KaTe GmbH

Integration package published:

KaTe Kafka Adapter

KaTe Kafka Adapter provides connectivity as publisher or subscriber of a Apache Kafka cluster with SAP Integration Suite or SAP Process Orchestration.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Corevist Inc

Integration package published:

Corevist Cloud Commerce Integration with SAP S/4HANA

Enable your customers to track orders and shipments; access invoices, quotes, and payment history; check account balance and available credit; and download business documents —directly from SAP ERP. Our customer portal streamlines operations, scales with your business, and fully aligns with the SAP Business Suite roadmap through direct SAP BTP integration. 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prezien Solutions Ltd

Validated Partner Use Case package published:

Editable, AI Assisted Document Generation in Sales and Service

Seamlessly generate, manage, and customise Word documents with AI assisted content for every sales, service, or HR interaction.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details. 

Stay tuned for further updates and information by the end of the next quarter!

entry.title: Connect to Public Cloud API in Node.js via Authorisation Code
entry.content:
Connecting to a Public Cloud API (from Business Accelerator Hub) via Node.js CAP application can be challenging. I hope this blog helps others attempting the same task.

 Prerequisites

• SAP BTP subaccount (Cloud Foundry)
• Service key for the target Public Cloud API
• XSUAA instance bound to your CAP app
• SAP BTP Destination service instance
• Bruno installed for manual testing

 

1. Confirm the API's Auth Options

First, check which authentication methods are available for the API you plan to consume.

In my example I connect to the Specification Management API, but the steps apply to any Public Cloud API.

If the OAuth 2.0 Access Code Flow is listed under “Authentication Methods”, the API supports the authorization-code flow. That flow confirms a user context and usually grants wider authorisations, such as update and POST requests.

(OAuth 2.0 Application Flow corresponds to the client-credentials flow.)

2. Connect manually with Bruno (Good blog on Bruno)

Because our API supports the auth-code flow, we first prove we can log in by calling it via Bruno.

Create a new collection and paste the base URI of your Public Cloud API instance (you’ll find it in the service key in your service instance of the saas application your connecting to).

When you send a test GET request you will probably receive an authorisation error. Configure OAuth 2.0 on the Auth tab

Select Grant type Authorisation Code

Then fill in the below fields using your service key from your service instance

  • Callback URL: <your Public Cloud API hostname>
  • Auth URL:** https://<subdomain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
  • Access-Token URL: https://<subdomain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Client ID / Secret: from the uaa section of the service key

Request the token, pick your identity provider, and Bruno will return an access token. Inspect the scopes there to see what you can do.

3. Connect to the API from Node.js

After the manual check we can now automate the flow in a Node.js service.

3.1 Destination setup

Create a destination that points to the same API endpoint and bind it to your app.

The required scopes and the audience (aud) value are visible inside the Bruno access token.

the Audience should start with the client id of your CAP application on BTP

Once the destination is saved, ensure your application is bound to the Destination service.

3.2 xs-security.json

Add every scope your service needs so the approuter, it will forward it in the user’s JWT: in my example i needed this scope.

{
  "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Spc_Write",
  "description": "Allow modification of specifications."
}

Also reference the scope in authorities (and role templates, if used) and assign the resulting role collection to your user.

3.3 Connect in code

Now time to connect to the API in the code itself.

First, obtain the user’s JWT. This helper below tries every possible source and falls back to LOCAL_USER_JWT in local development:

/** Try every source for a JWT and fall back to LOCAL_USER_JWT. */
function getJwt(capReq) {
  // CAP ≥ 7.3 keeps the raw token here
  if (capReq.user?.jwt) return capReq.user.jwt;

  // Plain HTTP / WebSocket headers
  const hdrs = capReq.http?.req?.headers || {};
  const token =
        hdrs['x-approuter-authorization'] ||   // CF WebSocket
        hdrs.authorization;                    // CF HTTP

  if (token) return token.replace(/^Bearer\s+/, '');
  // Last resort for local dev
  return process.env.LOCAL_USER_JWT;
}

3.3.1 Testing locally

When testing locally we don't have access to the user JWT so we can add it manually to the environment file so we can still test locally, below is the process to get the JWT for user testing (each token lasts about 7 to 8 hours)

Step 1 – Get an auth code
https://<instance>.eu10.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
?response_type=code
&client_id=<XSUAA client id of your deployed app>
&redirect_uri=http://localhost
&state=xyz

Paste your version into your browsers address bar, after login you will land on http://localhost/?code=<AUTH_CODE>&state=xyz. Copy the value of AUTH_CODE.

Step 2 – Exchange the code for a JWT

I made the Curl Request inside BAS itself. it will return a number of tokens, we only need the first and biggest one.

curl -v -X POST \
-u '<same client id>' \
--data-urlencode grant_type=authorization_code \
--data-urlencode code=<AUTH_CODE> \
--data-urlencode redirect_uri=http://localhost \
https://<instance>.eu10.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Save the token from the response into .env:

LOCAL_USER_JWT=<paste JWT here>

3.3.2 Call the destination with the JWT

const jwt = getJwt(req);
if (!jwt) req.reject(401, 'No user token found.');

const dest = await getDestination({
  destinationName: '<DestinationName>',
  jwt
});
await executeHttpRequest(dest, {
  method: 'POST',
  url: `/odata/v4/api/specification/v1/${EntityName}`,
  data: v
});

If you reach the destination but receive 401 Unauthorized, your JWT is missing the correct scope—ask the product team which one you need.

Other common pitfall is using the different XSUAA instances to get your JWT for the local run. use your applications XSUAA client since this is the same one your application will check the token against

And that’s it! You should now be able to read from and write to the Public Cloud API. For read-only workloads the client-credentials flow is usually simpler.

If I missed anything or you’d like more details, please comment below—happy to help.

 

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What’s the difference between authorization-code and client-credentials flows?
The authorization-code flow runs in a user context, so it can carry user-level scopes (read, write, update). The client-credentials flow operates with a technical user—perfect for background jobs or read-only integrations.

### Why do I get “401 Unauthorized” after deployment?
Your user’s JWT is probably missing the required scope (e.g., `Spc_Write`). Make sure the scope is listed in xs-security.json, mapped to a role template, and assigned via a role collection in the BTP cockpit.

### Which audience value belongs in my destination?
Use the client ID of your CAP application’s XSUAA instance; it usually starts with `sb-<appName>!t<number>`.

Node.jsSAP Cloud Identity ServicesAPISAP AuthenticatorSAP Fiori CloudSAP Cloud Application Programming ModelSAP Business Application StudioSAP Business Technology PlatformSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ExtensibilitySAP Business Accelerator HubJavaScriptAPI Management

entry.title: API Consumption - S/4 HANA Cloud,Public Edition and S/4 HANA Cloud,Private Edition
entry.content:
With the evolving SAP landscape and customers moving to S/4 HANA Cloud , our prime focus as a consultant is to follow a clean core approach.

SAP Business Accelerator Hub(https://api.sap.com/) is one such platform which helps us in discovering standard APIs,Events, Extensibility options

which we can consume in our S/4 HANA landscape to achieve end-to-end business functionality. This blog focusses mainly on the API consumption from

SAP Business Accelerator HUB and how it differs between the two of the S/4 HANA Cloud offerings - S/4 HANA Cloud,Public Edition and S/4 HANA Cloud,Private Edition.

We will first go through some of the basic differences and then focus on technically how we can use or consume these APIs in each of the environments.

 

S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition

Where to find?

 

Ready to use APIs available in Business Accelerator Hub(https://api.sap.com/) to be explored under the Tile -

 

Ready to use APIs available in Business Accelerator Hub(https://api.sap.com/) to be explored under the Tile –

 

Activation

 

Communication management based API Activation. APIs are part of a standard communication scenario. Communication scenario can be found from SAP Business Accelerator HUB as below –

 

 

SICF based API activation(OData V2)For OData V4 no specific activation is needed but the Service Group needs to be published. Not associated to any communication scenario.

 

 

Sandbox availability

“TryOut” option available in SAP Business Accelerator HUB for OData APIs to execute the CRUD methods with Sandbox data. Also you can deploy your own system to do tryout with your actual data of your S/4 HANA Cloud,Public Edition system.

 

 

 

No Tryout option available with any Sandbox. Also here the S/4 HANA system is in a private network and often there will be a Cloud Connector in between so TryOut options have been restricted for APIs for Private Edition

 

 


 

 

 

Consumption

API is consumed through a communication arrangement which is based on a standard communication scenario. Standard Fiori Application -   Communication Arrangements to be used for consumption.

For OData V2 API is activated through transaction code - /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

For OData V4 API no separates activation is required but service group needs to be published through transaction code - /n/IWFND/V4_ADMIN

Practice Guide

We will be using OData V2 - API - API_PRODUCT_SRV as an example to demonstrate the activation of the API in S/4 HANA Public Cloud and S/4 HANA Private Cloud.

API Link for Public Edition – https://api.sap.com/api/API_PRODUCT_SRV/overview

API Link for Private Edition - https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_PRODUCT_SRV_0001/overview

 

Scenario 1# Activating API in Public Cloud –

Navigate to the link https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PRODUCT_0002/overview  for the API or you can search the API as below.

In https://api.sap.com/ go to the tile SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Under APIs go to ODATA V2 and search with “Product” and open the one Product Master(A2X) which say ODATA V2 API and is “Active”  for usage.

 

From SAP Business Accelerator HUB you will get the SAP standard communication scenario for the API –

 

Here we will be creating arrangement for communication scenario – SAP_COM_009

Next create a communication user from the Fiori application “Create Communication User”.

 

Give a Username and you can use the “Propose Password” option or you can add your own password.

 

Next step is to create a communication system from the Fiori application “Communication Systems”

 

Click on “New” and provide a System ID and System Name –

 

 

Go to tab “Users for Inbound Communication” and assign the user you just created in the previous step using “Create Communication Users”.

 

 

For the ease of use you can check the checkbox “Inbound Only” which means you are not expecting any outbound communication (S/4HANA -> External) through this communication system and so you will not be asked to enter any user for Outbound communication user for this communication system.

Save the communication system.

Next step is to create the communication arrangement. Go to application “Communication Arrangements”

 

Click on “New”

In the following tab enter “SAP_COM_0009” as the standard communication scenario for API_PRODUCT_SRV.

 

Select the ‘Communication System'(from value help) which we have just created in the previous step. The assigned Communication User will now automatically appear in User Name field.

 

Click “Save” and it will activate your communication arrangement.

You can get your actual service URL for all Inbound Services associated, under the tab “Inbound Services” under the column “Service URL/Service Interface”.

Similarly all the Outbound services associated with this communication Scenario “SAP_COM_0009” will show under the tab “Outbound Services”.Outbound services are generally either SOAP Outbound services or IDOCs.

 

 

All the services that belong to the communication scenario “SAP_COM_0009” will be listed here under the tab “Inbound Service”.

Get the URL where the Service says “Product Master(A2X)” / OData V2”

 

The API link looks like this - https://<host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV

So this makes your API ready for consumption from the external system.

Get the service URL and test it from POSTMAN using the communication user that you created in the first step. In POSTMAN you can use it for Basic Authentication under Authorization.

You can call this URL from SAP Integration Suite using a OData V2 Adapter with basic authentication with the user you created in “Communication User” application.

Note – For Public cloud the way of activating a OData V2 service and OData V4 service are same unlike Private Cloud where there is a difference.

So for OData V4 the API will be also activated by a communication arrangement only as shown above and the URL will look like this - https://<host>/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_product/srvd_a2x/sap/product/0002/

Link to API Hub for the OData V4 API of Product Master - https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PRODUCT_0002/overview

 

Scenario 2# Activating API in Private Cloud –

First step same as Public cloud to find the API from https://api.sap.com/ .

Navigate to the link https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_PRODUCT_SRV_0001/overview for the API or you can search the API as below.

In https://api.sap.com/ go to the tile SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition

 

Under APIs go to ODATA V2 and search with “Product master” and open the one Product Master(A2X) which say ODATA V2 API.

 

 

Next Run Transaction Code - /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

Click on “Add Service”

Provide System Alias = “LOCAL” and under Technical Service name – “API_PRODUCT_SRV” and click on “Get Services”

 

Then select the service you want to activate and click on “Add Selected Services”

You can provide the package where you want to assign the service.

 

If you want to use OAuth authentication you have to check the checkbox “Enable OAuth for Service”

The next step is to activate the service as shown below. However it is recommended to go and activate the service from SICF transaction code.

 

Your OData V2 Activation is complete and you can test it from the Gateway. You can call this service now from SAP Integration Suite with a technical user who has the necessary roles.

 

 

Bonus Scenario # Now we will see how we can activate a similar OData V4 service of Product Master.

To find the OData V4 API go to https://api.sap.com/ and go to the node – “SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition”

:291522iD133E2FCB72EF160:

 

Under APIs->ODATA V4 -> Search Product

 

Select the “Odata V4” one which is “Active” and not “DEPRECATED”

 

Under API Resources -> Attributes you will find the Service Group Name which you need to publish.

 

Now Go to Transaction - /n/IWFND/V4_ADMIN and click on “Publish Service Groups”

 

Next enter the Service Group Name you got from Business accelerator Hub and click on “Get Service Groups”

 

 

Now your OData V4 API will be ready for consumption from Integration Suite.

entry.title: Connect & Go: Integration Updates for 2025/1
entry.content:
A seamlessly connected business is more crucial than ever as organizations pursue agility and innovation. Just like the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite that enable you to connect everything in your business, the Connect & Go series brings you a recap of the highlights for the product and how you can get started and integrate with confidence across SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

In the first half of 2025, SAP Integration Suite has delivered new features and enhancements, reaffirming its role as the AI-assisted IPaaS for seamless connectivity, automation, and data-driven decision making. As you dive into this post, you will explore AI updates, expanded capabilities for migration to Integration Suite, latest additions to third-party connectivity and API-centric and event-driven integration. Enjoy!

 

💎AI Updates

SAP Integration Suite introduced a new user experience that empowers developers to use AI capabilities more effectively in their development, configuration, migration, testing, and workload monitoring tasks. This revamped interface aimed to enhance overall productivity and operational excellence, with a consolidated landing page offering a unified view and central activation control for all AI features.

AI-assisted API trends and predictions

Now organizations can leverage AI and machine learning to analyze historical API call volumes and forecast trends and provide insights into traffic patterns.

Deviations from the normal usage or performance in API traffic often indicate security threats, system malfunctions, or other issues that might result in financial loss. This feature addresses this challenge through ML-based anomaly detection, which identifies unusual or abnormal behaviors by continuously monitoring the API traffic. Upon detection of anomalies in API Call Volume, API Error count, and API Response time, the system provides notifications and insights to support investigating the cause and analyzing the impact, as well as recommendations on probable remedial actions. Customers can customize options to select specific API proxies, choose the prediction duration, and set the frequency according to their preferences. With this feature they can proactively adjust their strategies, improving their resilience and competitive edge to maintain the security, reliability, and performance of their APIs. Read more: Leveraging API Traffic Predictions in SAP Integration Suite.

 

📦SAP Business Accelerator Hub News

That the SAP Business Accelerator Hub is the one-stop-shop for pre-built business accelerators you already know. But now, it includes Joule-based discovery and reuse of artifacts to help customers and partners find the right content faster and more intuitively. Learn more about the feature (currently in beta). For an even smoother experience, the hub provides quick access to AI-specific APIs, events, and integrations in a dedicated space. 

And last but not least, the hub now includes a wide range of content for Data Products. Users can explore these products within SAP Business Data Cloud that are built to help businesses manage, analyze, and maximize the value of their data. Offerings include curated datasets, data integration tools, and analytics applications, all designed to support informed, data-driven decision-making.

 

💡 Migration and Modernization

Support of Exactly-Once-In-Order scenario (EOIO)

EOIO is essential in business scenarios where the receiving system requires messages to be received exactly once in the correct order, such as banking operations like deposits and withdrawals, where message order is critical. This functionality supports EOIO via exclusive queues in Cloud Integration and exclusive or partitioned queues using Advance Event Mesh. It ensures no duplicate messages or message loss, as well as the preservation of message sequence during processing.

To learn more about EOIO and how to configure Java Message Service (JMS) adapter, check out New integration flow design guidelines covering Exactly Once In Order pattern, the documentation, and the sample integration flows on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. For AEM, see Exactly once in order (EOIO) for Group messages using Advanced Event Mesh.

Clean Core Modernization Recommendations for IDOCs

New modernization recommendations now include evaluations of different IDOC types in integration scenarios. The respective SOAP/ODATA APIs and Business Events are recommended according to the content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Customers can leverage these recommendations during migration to obtain risk and cost reduction when adopting a clean core approach. The additional information is reported in the full evaluation result spreadsheet, learn how to extract the data with a Data Extraction Request or follow the tutorial.

 

🔗Third-party Connectivity

In this first half of 2025 we’ve expanded our ecosystem to help customers integrate across platforms effortlessly. A new set of integration adapters include: IBM MQ (REST, JMS), SAP LeanIX, Google Cloud Storage, Google Pub/Sub, Google BigQuery, Salesforce Pub/Sub, DocuSign, MS 365 Teams, MS 365 OneDrive, Shopify, and more. Additionally, the Remote Function Call (RFC) sender and receiver adapter was made available in Edge Integration Cell to allow direct, on-premise communication with SAP and third-party systems without relying on the SAP Cloud Connector, allowing these systems to initiate RFC calls.

Data Space Integration

The Data Space Integration feature of SAP Integration Suite was made available in Q2 enabling participants within a data space, such as Catena-X, to exchange data with one another in a secure, reliable, and sovereign way. It provides straightforward integration between SAP and non-SAP applications and offers a unified, user-friendly approach to configuring and monitoring data exchanges within the data space, all using the SAP Integration Suite - learn more.

 

🚀API-centric and event-driven integration

Single-source API and events catalog for developers

The API business hub enterprise is now the Developer Hub: a centralized catalog designed for application developers to easily discover, search, and utilize APIs and business events published by their organizations. Customers can benefit from building an extensive company-wide repository of APIs and events, including thorough documentation and code examples, as well as new features like self-registration and analytics. All to empower developers to efficiently integrate various systems and applications. Start exploring: check out how you can Discover S/4HANA Cloud Events in SAP Developer Hub and Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog; and read the documentation for APIs and Events. 

 

SAP SuccessFactors events on SAP Business Accelerator Hub​

Explore the new events for SAP SuccessFactors published on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Stay tuned for more events to come!

Event add-on for SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA

This add-on for SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC allows the creation of custom events based on different trigger types. These events can be both notification and data events and allow for loosely coupled, highly scalable, real-time event-driven business architectures powered by SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Users can easily configure the add-on and create their custom extensible events with or without code, as well as leverage templates for selected events.

Find all relevant details for this exciting update in the blog: The E in Event-Driven Architecture: Introducing SAP’s new Event-Add for ERP.

 

The next edition of Connect & Go is scheduled for September/October 2025. Until then:

- Stay informed | Read the Related Updates:

  • Generative AI-based script optimization – Q1 25
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub domains take centerstage – Q1 25
  • New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub - Q2 25
  • Integration Advisor – multi-payload support for MIG creation - Q3 25
  • Update Version of an Integration Flow Component - Q3 25
  • Partner Directory Accelerator Tool for Pipeline Concept  – Q3 25

- Stay in Touch:

  • Join the monthly update webinars 
  • Interact in the Integration group
  • Subscribe to the Newsletter

entry.title: Extending SBPA Processes with APIs and Using Them in Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Introduction

In the first blog post, we built and deployed a functional workflow in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) and connected it to Integrated Product Development (IPD). Now, it’s time to take the next step: extending your process with API integrations.

In this article, we will explore how to set up and use APIs within SBPA processes to enrich your workflows with collaboration data and advanced functionality from IPD. You will learn how to:

  • Add and configure destinations
  • Set up and manage Actions in SBPA
  • Include and execute API calls directly within your process

Our specific goal in this post is to retrieve the Collaboration Name via an API call and make it visible inside a form within your workflow. By the end of this post, you’ll know how to connect SBPA to IPD Collaboration APIs and enrich your workflow with live collaboration data.

Prerequisite: You need a functional IPD workflow in SBPA. If you haven’t created one yet, you can follow my step-by-step guide to build a process in SBPA and integrate it into IPD.

 

Guide

Step 1: Register the EDP_Collaboration destination in SBPA

  1. From the SAP Build (SBPA) Lobby, open Control Tower.
  2. Under Backend Configuration, click Destinations.

Step 2: Select the EPD_Collaboration destination

  1. To add a destination, click the Add button.
  2. Choose the EPD_Collaboration destination from the list.
  3. Click Next to proceed to Associate Environments.

Step 3: Associate the destination with environments

  1. In the Associate Environments step, choose how the destination should be available:
    • All Environments: makes the destination usable in every SBPA environment.
    • Specific Environments: select only the environments where this destination should be active.
  2. Click Add Destination to save.

Step 4: Create an Actions project

  1. In the SBPA Lobby, go to Connectors → Actions.
  2. Click Create (top-right).

Step 5: Import the IPD Collaboration API from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

  1. In your Actions project, click Choose an API Source and select SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
  2. In the search bar, type Collaboration and select Collaboration (ODATA) under SAP Integrated Product Development (IPD).
  3. Import the API into your Actions project.

Step 6: Create the Action Project

  1. After selecting the Collaboration API, you will be prompted to create an Action project.
  2. The Project Name and Description are prefilled with default values. You can keep them or adjust as needed.
  3. Click Create to finalize the Action project setup.

Step 7: Add Available API Actions

  1. A list of API actions from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub will appear.
  2. Select the APIs you want to use for your collaboration scenario (for this guide: Retrieve a collaboration for the specified collaboration ID).
  3. Confirm with Add.
  4. All chosen API actions will now be available in your Action Project.

Tip: If you want to add an API later, open the Available Actions window using the Add button next to Actions.

Step 8: Add missing "From" header for IPD APIs

For many IPD APIs, an additional From header is required. This header is not automatically included and must be configured manually.

  1. Open Add under the Parameters section.
  2. Configure the parameter as follows:
    • Key: From
    • Mandatory: Yes
    • Parameter: header
    • Type: string
  3. Add the parameter.

To check whether an API requires the From header, refer to the SAP API Business Hub – EPD Collaboration API.

Step 9: Save, Release and Publish the Action Project

  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Release.
  3. Click Publish.

Step 10: Move back into the process

After releasing and publishing the Action Project, return to your process model to continue the setup.

  1. Open your project in SAP Build Process Automation.
  2. Switch the project from Deployed back to Editable mode by selecting the dropdown and choosing Editable.
    • This allows you to make changes to the process again.
  3. In the project overview, click on your process (e.g., IPD Process) to reopen it in the process editor.

Step 11: Add the CollaborationID and TechnicalUser as custom variables

  1. Under Process Details, configure Custom Variables.
  2. Add CollaborationID and technicalUser as custom variables.
    • You can either set the technicalUser directly as an initial variable or define it later in a Script Task.

Step 12: Extract the Collaboration ID from the Business Key

  1. Add a Script Task to your process.
  2. In the script editor, insert the following JavaScript logic:
// get Business Key
var businessKey = $.context.startEvent.businessKey;

// delete "Collaboration"
var collaborationId = businessKey.replace("Collaboration", "");

// Write in Context
$.context.custom.collaborationid = collaborationId;
$.context.custom.technicalUser = "your@mail.com"; // enter the technical User Mail

Step 13: Add Collaboration Actions

  1. After the Script Task, add a new element and select Action.
  2. This connects the Collaboration API (configured earlier) directly to your process.
  3. You can either reuse an existing action from your library or add a new one by selecting Browse All Actions.
  4. In the Action Library, search for and select the Collaboration API you want to use (In this guide the API: Retrieves a collaboration for the specified collaboration ID).

Step 14: Configure Destination Variable

  1. Choose a Name for the step.
  2. Under Destination Variable, create or select the variable that points to your Collaboration destination.
  3. When deploying the workflow, set this destination variable to EPD_Collaboration.

Step 15: Configure API Inputs

  1. In the workflow action settings, navigate to the Inputs tab.
  2. For the field From, select the variable technicalUser.
  3. For the field ID, select the variable CollaborationID.

This maps your process data to the API request. All responses from the API call are available as variables, which can be mapped and used in the input fields.

Step 16: Add result from API to your Form

  1. Open the form.
  2. Click Open Editor.
  3. Add a Text Field and name it Collaboration Name.
  4. Save the form.
  5. Return to the Process and open the Input tab of the form.
  6. Add the Collaboration Name into the field.

Step 17: Save, Release, and Deploy the Project

  1. Save your project after adding the API steps.
  2. Release the project to make it ready for deployment.
  3. During deployment, set the Destination Variable to the EPD_Collaboration destination.

Step 18: Start the Workflow in SAP IPD

  1. Open the My Collaborations app in SAP IPD.
  2. Select a collaboration where the workflow is already linked to the collaboration type (see how to do that: SBPA Guide)
  3. Add the workflow to the collaboration.

Note: This requires that you have completed all configuration steps from the SBPA guide, including setting up the Business Key, defining the Subject, and configuring the Workflow Properties and other prerequisites. Since the process was already configured, deploying it in SBPA only creates a new version of the workflow. When you start the workflow in IPD, the newly deployed version will be executed. 

Step 20: Check the updated form in My Inbox

  1. Open the My Inbox app in SAP IPD.
  2. Select the task created by your workflow.
  3. In the form, locate the text field Collaboration Name.
  4. The field now displays the Collaboration Name that was returned by the API.

Optional: Monitor the Workflow

  1. Use the Monitor Workflow app to track execution details.
  2. In the monitoring view, verify that the API call is executed successfully and inspect the request and response data if needed.

Conclusion

Great work! You have successfully extended your workflow in SAP Build Process Automation by integrating APIs and connected it with Integrated Product Development. With the Collaboration API in place, your process can now dynamically retrieve and display collaboration data, such as the Collaboration Name, directly inside your forms.

Beyond this example, you can use many other APIs from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to enrich your workflows with additional business data, collaboration features, or system integrations. This demonstrates how SBPA can go beyond simple workflow automation and become a powerful integration layer for IPD.

Alexandra Rong,
Business Process Consultant for SAP IPD,
alexandra.rong@sap.com

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q3 ’25)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to bring you the latest updates and maintain the momentum of delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, explore the newest content packages (particularly APIs and Integrations) released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q3 2025, along with their high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration packages published:

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with Third Party CSR Vendor - Benevity

This package supports integration of business processes in your on-demand SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central system with your Third Party CSR Vendor - Benevity.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with Third Party Benefits Vendor - Bswift

This package supports integration of business processes in your on-demand SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central system with your Third Party Benefits Vendor - Bswift

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PiLog India Pvt Ltd

API package published:

PiLog Data Profiling

PiLog Data Profiling automates the process of examining and analyzing datasets to provide insights into their structure, quality, and content. It generates detailed HTML reports that highlight anomalies, missing values, patterns, and inconsistencies, enabling organizations to ensure data accuracy and suitability for use. This process is critical for data quality management, supporting data cleansing, validation, and preparation for advanced analytics or decision-making. By automating profiling, it minimizes manual effort, reduces errors, and accelerates data assessment for real-time insights.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ComZam Datentechnik GmbH

Integration package published:

SAP Customer Checkout Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

The integration of SAP Customer Checkout with SAP S/4HANA Cloud features the replication of relevant master data to SAP Customer Checkout as well as posting Point of Sale transactions to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cobrainer GmbH

Integration package published:

SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub Integration with Cobrainer Job Architecture

This package contains artifacts that enable the various integration scenarios between SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub and Cobrainer Job Architecture.

Navigate to the package mentioned above to know more about those supported integration scenarios.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figaf ApS

Integration packages published:

Figaf Azure DevOps Integration

The "Figaf Azure DevOps Integration" package provides a template for integrating Figaf and Azure. It includes highly configurable iFlows processing Figaf webhooks for ticket/transport events to Azure actions, update Figaf based on Azure changes, and a utility for searching issues.

Figaf Jira Integration

The "Figaf Jira Integration" package provides a template for integrating Figaf and Jira. It includes highly configurable iFlows processing Figaf webhooks for ticket/transport events to Jira actions, update Figaf based on Jira changes, and a utility for searching issues.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sodales Solutions Inc 

Integration packages published:

Microsoft Booking Integration with SAP HANA

This package integrates with the Microsoft Booking API, enabling you to create and retrieve booking information. It helps streamline workflows, reduce manual tasks, and provide a unified experience across platforms.

Microsoft Teams Integration with SAP HANA

The Microsoft Graph API for Teams chat allows businesses to automate and integrate their Microsoft Teams chat functionality with a wide range of other applications and services. With this API, developers can programmatically send, receive, and manage chat messages within Teams, enabling seamless communication between systems and users.

Microsoft Mail Integration with SAP HANA

The Microsoft Graph API for email enables businesses to automate and integrate their email systems with various applications and services. It allows developers to programmatically access and manage email messages, folders, and attachments in Office 365 mailboxes. 

SAP SFTP Employee Data with PGP Encryption-Decryption to SAP HANA

This package automates retrieving a CSV file from an SFTP server, encrypting specific fields, transforming the data into JSON format, and sending it to a defined receiver, such as SAP HANA. It ensures secure handling of sensitive data while converting it into a standardized format for downstream systems.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rojo Consultancy B.V.

Integration package published:

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Zycus

Zycus Accelerator for SAP S/4HANA delivers a fully pre-configured integration solution to help you replicate Cost Center from S/4 HANA to Zycus.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration package published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP S4HANA

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Business Partner Replication Adapter for SAP S/4HANA seamlessly integrates supplier master data between SAP S/4HANA and Sirion CLM.  The Business Partner records initiated or updated in SAP S/4HANA are automatically replicated as Supplier profiles in Sirion CLM, ensuring alignment and consistency throughout sourcing and contracting workflows.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPS Commerce

Integration package published:

SPS Commerce Supplier Integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

The SPS Commerce Fulfillment Connector for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables businesses to effortlessly automate and optimize electronic data interchange (EDI) with trading partners, streamlining order-to-cash processes and enhancing supply chain visibility. This cloud-based solution offers easy configuration, scalable performance, and a single connection to thousands of retailers, vendors, and logistics providers, reducing manual work and errors while supporting business growth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PriceFx AG

Integration packages published:

 

SAP S/4HANA Exchange Rate Integration with Pricefx
 
This integration package contains integration flows to replicate exchange rates from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA and Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.
 
SAP S/4HANA Exchange Rate Mapping with Pricefx
 
This integration package contains sample mappings required to replicate exchange rates from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx.
 
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Microsoft Corporation

Integration package published:

 

Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP ERP and S/4HANA
 
Use the RISE certified Microsoft Sentinel solution for SAP applications to monitor your SAP systems any-premise with Microsoft Sentinel, detecting sophisticated threats throughout the business logic and application layers of your SAP applications. Use of Microsoft Security Copilot ensure fast time to response on SAP incidents.
 
The Microsoft Sentinel agentless data connector for SAP uses the SAP Cloud Connector and SAP Integration Suite to connect to your SAP Netweaver based systems and pull logs from it, as shown in the attached image.
 
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details. 

Stay tuned for further interesting updates and information by the end of this quarter!

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: What’s New in Q3/2025
entry.content:
Another quarter has come to an end and it’s time for us to review the most recent updates and improvements delivered to SAP Integration Suite. Whether you’re an integration architect, developer, or business leader, these new features are designed to help you get the most out of your hybrid landscape. Read on to discover what’s new and how these enhancements can support your organisation’s integration goals.

Message status overview monitoring for large tenants

One awaited featured has been released to the Cloud Integration capability of SAP Integration Suite to improve Message Status Overview monitoring and support larger time intervals through data preaggregation. Now the monitoring option can be utilized for extended periods on tenants with a high volume of message processing logs.

Artificial Intelligence

AI-based integration flow generation with mediation steps

Earlier this year, the generation of integration flows with the assistance of AI was made available to customers. This enable users to generate an integration flow with sender and receiver systems based on a scenario description provided in natural language. You can read more about it here.

In Q3/2025 we have enhanced this feature to include additional mediation steps. This means that now, users can provide even more detailed scenarios and the tool identifies the details and generates a comprehensive integration flow with the described components, systems, and actions. Get a first glimpse into how to use this feature in the blog post: SAP Integration Suite – Generative AI based Integration Flow Generation with Mediation Steps.

 Business Accelerators

Joule copilot-based conversational search and discovery of content

Joule has arrived at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub in Q3 to help users find the right content throughconversational context-based search. The Joule-based discovery provides user guidance to search different artifacts and access cohesive information for other relevant sources, such as help documentation.

Note: The feature is currently in the beta phase and you can find more details on availability here.  

Connectivity and Adapters

The latest releases of SAP Integration Suite introduced a new array of adapters and connectors designed to streamline and enhance connectivity, improve data flow, and foster a more integrated digital landscape for businesses leveraging SAP solutions.

AI adapter

The recently released AI adapter for SAP Integration Suite functions as a receiver adapter and accelerates connectivity to the Generative AI Hub (SAP AI Core) and other AI providers, as needed by customer (BYOM).

 The benefit of this adapter is that it increases connectivity options to integrate with both SAP and third-party systems and applications. How it works: after configuration, data exchange is performed at runtime where the SAP Integration Suite tenant sends the operation request to GenAIHub. The AI Adapter works on the request and sends the data back to the SAP Integration Suite tenant.

Find the AI Adapter at SAP Business Accelerator Hub, read the help documentation, and check out the short demo below:

Further adapters released in Q3 include:

IBM MQ adapter using JMS protocol: client certificate supportSupports Client Certificate based authentication for JMS Variants. A new version 1.3.0 of the IBM MQ adapter supports Client Certificate based authentication for JMS Variants.- Configure the IBM MQ JMS Receiver Adapter
IBM MQ adapter using JMS protocol: client certificate supportSupports On-Premise Connectivity via Cloud Connector for JMS Variants. A new version 1.4.0 of the IBM MQ adapter supports On-Premise Connectivity via Cloud Connector for JMS Variants.

- Configure the IBM MQ JMS Sender Adapter

- Configure the IBM MQ JMS Receiver Adapter
BigCommerce adapterFacilitates the connectivity to BigCommerce GraphQL & REST Admin APIs, accelerates the implementation time and reduces complexity of connecting to BigCommerce. This adapter is available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

- BigCommerce Adapter at SAP Business Accelerator Hub

- BigCommerce Receiver Adapter

🚀🚀Discover the latest content packages, including APIs and Integrations, released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q3 2025. These releases come with high-level details to help you understand their features and benefits.

API-Centric & Event-Driven Integrations

Support for reusable API artifacts in Edge Integration Cell

Customers can now leverage components, endpoints, and services across multiple applications, projects, or contexts, without having to perform significant adaptations. The reusability of API artifacts enables companies to implement a centralized security and traffic management system for exposed APIs. It not only enhances compliance and reduces misconfiguration risks but also significantly lowers both the total cost of development and ownership by eliminating redundant configurations and promoting the reuse of existing API artifacts, which means additional API calls are avoided, leading to cost savings. Learn more.

 

Watch the demo from this year's Devtoberfest - Session "Next generation hybrid integration with Edge Integration Cell":

Integration of Advanced Event Mesh with SAP Cloud Application Event Hub

The latest updates allow you to connect your SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh system to SAP Cloud Application Event Hub for the consumption and exposure of events.

SAP Cloud Application Event Hub creates a clear, user-friendly, and adaptable way to easily exchange business events across SAP's cloud landscape. The solution is a key part of SAP's event-driven architecture. With the integration, you can access events from various SAP business systems, published via the hub, directly on SAP Integration Suite's advanced event mesh.

Migration & Modernization

ABAP proxy generation from service interfaces for current users of SAP Process Orchestration

SAP Integration Suite now supports the implementation of existing use cases for the Enterprise Services Repository using an efficient API-first development methodology. This assists users in importing the service interface artifacts from their PI/PO systems to the Cloud Integration workspace. The feature includes the ability to generate and update ABAP Proxies (non-language-specific interface descriptions in Web Services Description Language converted into executable interfaces) in SAP backend or S/4 HANA systems by connecting them to the SAP Integration Suite tenant and it’s available in the multi-cloud environment. Find more details and the prerequisites here.

Migration assessment: Clean core modernization recommendations for BAPIs

This extension of the Migration Assessment capability in SAP Integration Suite makes it possible for users to analyse used BAPIs in SAP Process Orchestration. Additionally, they receive recommendations available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to transition from legacy content and adopt a clean core approach, e.g. replacing RFCs (BAPIs) with SOAP/OData APIs, IDOCs with Business Events, Alert Rules by SAP Cloud ALM, and more. Explore all modernization recommendations covered in each area.

Dive Deeper

New learning offerings available

Accelerating Hybrid Integrations with SAP Integration Suite on SUSE Rancher Set up, deploy, and operate Edge Integration Cell on SUSE Rancher Kubernetes EngineTime investment 3h, Record of achievement
Accelerating Hybrid Integrations with SAP Integration Suite on RedHat OpenShift Set up, deploy, and run an Edge Integration Cell on Red Hat OpenShiftTime investment 3h, Record of achievement
Exploring Enterprise Automation with SAPUnderstand how SAP Signavio, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite work together to improve efficiency and responsiveness Time investment 1.5h, Record of achievement

What’s next?

  • Integration @ Devtoberfest: watch all sessions
  • Join us at SAP TechEd 2025: explore the Integration track and register

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q4 ’25)
entry.content:
As a continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to bring you the latest updates and maintain the momentum of delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, explore the newest content packages (particularly APIs, Integrations and SAP Partner Validated Use Case) published on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q4 2025, along with their high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Akeneo

Integration package published:

Akeneo PIM Data Model Generator

Akeneo PIM's flexibility is one of its strengths, but it means every instance has a unique data model. Manually recreating this structure for integration can be a complex and time-consuming task. Akeneo PIM Data Model Generator automates this process entirely. By connecting to your Akeneo instance, it automatically generates a precise integration data contract that serves as the perfect blueprint for product synchronization.

valantic Digital Finance GmbH

SAP Validated Partner Use Case published:

Business Insight Framework for SAP Analytics Cloud

Valantic's SAP Analytics Cloud-asset serves as an accelerator that simplifies SAP Analytics Cloud dashboard creation, cutting costs and resource use across various lines of business. It offers a shared framework for SAP Analytics Cloud stories, allowing developers to concentrate on customization. This boosts efficiency, speeds up development, and ensures uniformity in design and functionality across dashboards.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration package published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP S/4HANA OA

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Outline Agreement Replication Adapter for SAP S/4HANA enables seamless integration of purchase contract requests between SAP S/4HANA and Sirion CLM.

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration package published:

WorkOrder Management Integration between ManWinWin and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Enables seamless data exchange between ManWinWin and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition to automate Work Order Management processes. This integration allows maintenance teams to capture and manage work order details in ManWinWin and automatically synchronize them with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, ensuring operational efficiency, data consistency, and reduced manual effort.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Overall Summary of the Year 2025:

In the year 2025, we witnessed significant progress in the expansion of our technological and business ecosystems. One of the noteworthy achievements is the publication of 37 new partner packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. These packages encompass a variety of Integrations, APIs, and Validated Partner Use Cases, all designed to enhance business operations, accelerate growth, and foster innovation.

Thank you for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting content in the future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details.

entry.title: SAP ORD Made Simple: What It Does and Why It’s Crucial in your SAP Landscape + Purpose and Value
entry.content:
ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is a protocol and metadata specification developed  to enable standardized discovery and integration of business services, APIs, events, and other resources across distributed systems across SAP Products.


Find out more details on ORD : https://open-resource-discovery.org/introduction
Let’s break down what ORD is, why we created it, where it fits into your architecture, and how it adds value across your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery)?

Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is a standardized protocol for metadata discovery that allows SAP applications and services to self‑describe their APIs, events, entity types, and other exposed resources. It provides a consistent, uniform structure for documentation, discovery, versioning, taxonomy, and lifecycle metadata.

Where ORD is Used in SAP

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Core component for service discovery and consumption
  • SAP S/4HANA Public & Private Cloud: Extensions and APIs are discoverable via ORD
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) : Consumer & Publisher of ORD Metadata that requires in Data Products
  • SAP Integration Suite: Uses ORD for automatic API discovery
  • SAP Graph: Built on top of ORD for unified data access

ORD essentially acts as a "catalog of catalogs," making SAP’s complex ecosystem more navigable and integration-friendly.

How to Know If an SAP API Supports ORD (Open Resource Discovery)

One of the simplest and most reliable ways to verify whether an API supports Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is to check for the ORD ID directly on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com).

When an API is published with ORD metadata from each SAP Product, automatically displays an “ORD ID” in the API details pane. This confirms that the API is fully enabled for the standardized metadata structure provided by ORD, meaning it can be seamlessly discovered, crawled, and consumed by systems such as SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).

Where to Find the ORD ID (Screenshot Provided)

In the API detail screen — typically under Overview — you will see a section that lists:

  • Direction (Inbound / Outbound)
  • ORD ID (a unique standardized identifier representing the ORD resource)

In the example image provided, the lower-right information box contains:

  • Direction: Inbound
  • ORD ID: sap.s4:apiResource...


This clearly indicates that:

The API is published with ORD metadata and is ready for integration with SAP Business Data Cloud and its Data Products framework.

Why This Matters

Having an ORD-enabled API means:

  • Your integration can rely on standardized metadata such as entity types, relations, events, and API capabilities (as defined in SAP’s ORD specification).
  • SAP Business Data Cloud can easily discover and interpret the API’s metadata for creating or connecting to Data Products.
  • You avoid custom mapping or manual documentation because ORD ensures consistent structure and discoverability across SAP systems.

This is part of the broader SAP strategy — documented through the Open Resource Discovery protocol — to harmonize how APIs and services describe themselves across the SAP ecosystem.

Why ORD is Important in SAP

  1. Unified Integration: ORD provides a single, standardized way to describe and discover all integration resources (APIs, events, entity types) across SAP’s diverse product portfolio

  2. Automation: It enables automated discovery and consumption of services without manual documentation or configuration, significantly reducing integration effort

  3. Metadata Consistency: ORD ensures consistent metadata across systems, making it easier for developers and integration tools to understand and consume services

  4. Decentralization: Each application can expose its own ORD document describing its capabilities, eliminating the need for central catalogs that quickly become outdated

  5. Ecosystem Enablement: Partners, customers, and third-party tools can automatically discover what integration points are available in their SAP landscape

SAP Provides an Open‑Source ORD Repository for CAP Developers

To accelerate adoption of Open Resource Discovery (ORD) across custom applications, SAP also provides an open‑source GitHub repository that enables developers to implement ORD using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). This repository offers reference implementations, examples, and tooling support so developers, partners and customers can easily expose ORD‑compliant metadata from their own services.


https://github.com/cap-js/ord
 
Demo Application provides ORD

This makes it straightforward for CAP‑based applications to:

  • Self‑describe APIs, events, business objects, and data products
  • Register and publish metadata consistently with SAP’s ORD standard
  • Integrate cleanly into the broader SAP ecosystem, including SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Graph, and SAP BTP services

 

Conclusion

SAP ORD (Open Resource Discovery) is one of the most important but often overlooked components in SAP’s modern integration strategy. By standardizing metadata discovery, enabling automated publication, and providing a consistent structure across SAP products, ORD delivers massive value for architects, developers, and business teams.

If you’re working with SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Databricks or any other SAP landscapes, understanding and leveraging ORD is no longer optional—it’s essential.

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente, trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte de los SAP Champions que lideran la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastiano Marchesini,

Me complace informarles que yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.🚀

Así que sí…

Pueden preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación – Ejecución de implementaciones end-to-end de SAP S/4HANA y programas de transformación a gran escala, permitiendo a las organizaciones modernizar sus procesos de negocio centrales, estandarizar operaciones globales y adoptar capacidades digitales de nueva generación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial – Procesos, artefactos y estrategias para la arquitectura empresarial de SAP basada en TOGAF.

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablando de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️ RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍 Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: Accelerating SAP ICSM and IRT integration with pre-packaged sample content
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management (ICSM) with IRT/RTSM systems is essential for running efficient clinical trials but many teams still end up reinventing the same integration patterns.

To help the ecosystem move faster, sample integration content for ICSM is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, providing a practical starting point for building integrations between ICSM and IRT systems using SAP Integration Suite.

This post introduces the content, highlights the supported ICSM-IRT scenarios and explains how partners and customers can reuse and extend these building blocks to accelerate real‑world integrations.

Business value

Instead of starting from scratch, partners and customer can now:

  • Reuse proven integration patterns
  • Accelerate ICSM-IRT implementations
  • Focus on value‑adding extensions rather than plumbing
  • Align with SAP‑recommended architecture and standards

The content is intentionally modular, making it easier to adapt to different IRT systems or extend with additional scenarios.

What's Included

The published package SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management-Sample Integration with IRT demonstrates end-to-end integration scenarios across both planning and operations, based on GS1 Clinical Trials standards (3.6) and SAP-recommended integration patterns.

How to Consume the Accelerator Content

This can be consumed using SAP’s standard process for pre-packaged integration content: The pre-packaged integration content in SAP integration Suite is a set of Integration flows (iflows) leveraging the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to integrate IRT and SAP ICSM smoothly. This prepackage content takes care of many aspects of integration; some of these are as follows:

  1. Connect the IRT system to SAP Integration Suite artifacts via API management.
  2. Transformation of message payload from GS1 to ICSM and vice versa.
  3. Seamless integration of SAP Event Mesh to take an action against the events raised from ICSM (e.g. sales order created; Inventory report executed etc.).

This pre-package integration content supports the following interfaces for planning and operation in ICSM:

Interfaces for ICSM Planning

  1. Study Information
  2. IRT actuals and Master

Interfaces for ICSM Operations

  1. Inventory Report
  2. Shipment request and confirmation
  3. Dispatch Advice
  4. Receiving Advice
  5. Dispensation Advice
  6. Kit Status Change (Inbound/Outbound)

Below are the steps to consume the pre-packaged integration content:

Step 1 - Discover the package

Navigate to your SAP Integration Suite - Integration - Discover tab and search for the package "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management Integration with IRT"

Below is the diagram shows the navigation steps.

                                                                                                              Fig: Integration Suite

Select the package and click copy to move it into your design workspace.  

Note: As with all standard packages, once you copy it, modifications in your workplace do not auto-inherit future SAP updates- SAP recommends keeping custom changes separate.

Step 2 - Review the Artifacts

Review each artifact integration flows and configure the user credentials for ICSM APIs and OAuth2 Client Credentials for event mesh using its service key values.

Step 3 - Configure the defined externalized parameters in each artifact

Update the values in each of the defined externalized parameters in each of the artifact integration flows.

The integration flows combine API-based and Event-driven approaches using SAP Integration Suite, SAP API Management, SAP Event Mesh and Cloud Connector, reflecting a reference architecture suitable for real-world landscapes.

Reference, not a Black Box

This is sample/accelerator content. Its purpose is to illustrate integration approaches and provide reusable building blocks - not to replace product features.
